def: n. m. A gamester; gambler.

( = S. parishkṛita), Clear, fair, serene (as the sky); clean, polished, bright; pure; fair, honest, candid
more: s. m. 1. (for پهرچها) Clearing away or dispersion (of clouds or of a multitude of people); fair weather. 2. (from پهرچانا) Decision, definitive sentence (of a judge).

more: v. a. To decide, to give a final sentence.

def: s.m. Clearance (as of the sky, &c.); dispersion (of clouds, or of a multitude of people); fair weather; settlement; liquidation; adjustment; decision, definitive sentence (of a judge); acquittal, release, discharge; — adj. ( = S. parishkṛita), Clear, fair, serene (as the sky); clean, polished, bright; pure; fair, honest, candid
more: n. m. S. परि all round, छो to divide. See فيصله 1. Clearing away; dispersion (clouds, people). 2. Acquittal; release; discharge. pharchhā'nā, pharchhā karnā , See فيصله کرنا To sweep; wipe off; clean; clear.
adj. Pure, honest, fair, candid, fair (not cloudy). پهرچهاکرنا pharchhā-karnā, v. a. To clean, to settle, to sweep.

more: v. a. To clean, to wipe, to settle, to clear away (the clouds, &c.).

more: n. f. S. फलक A small shield or buckler pharī gat'kā , n. m. Cudgel and shield.
s. f. One of several fishes.

s.f. A slab, block (of stone), a large stone (the poet Saudā says: Agar Saudā ko ćherā hai, to laṛko mol-lo phaṛiyāṅ; tasallī mujh dīwāne-ko na ho jholī-ke pattharoṅ-se).

n. m. 1. A retailer of goods. 2. Keeper of a gambling house or table.
s. f. 1. A kind of bordered vestment worn by Hindūs. 2. A contractor for reaping.

def: s.m. One who takes a contract for reaping.
(from پهڙ) s. m. 1. A pedler, a retailer. 2. The keeper of a gaming house or dice table.
s.m. The keeper of a gaming house or dice table; — a retailer of goods; pedlar, huckster.
s.f. = phaṛak, q.v. s.m. A shield ( = phar).
v. a. Caus. of پهرَکنا q. v.
v.t. To cause to flutter; to put in a flutter to cause convulsive motion in the muscles; to cause to throb, &c.; — (met.) to shew; to show off, to make a display.
more: (caus. of پهڙکنا) v. a. 1. To cause to flutter, to cause convulsive motion in the muscles, &c. 2. ( met. ) To show.

s.m. Pomp, show, display ( = dikhāʼo).
s.f. A coarse screen, a partition.

more: ( स्फाटन or caus. of پهٿنا q. v.) v. a. To tear, rend, split, break, cleave (wood, &c.), to tear open, burst, rip.

v. a. (dakh.) To read: see پڙهنا paṛhnā.
s.f. A mixture of parched rice and barley used as food (see ćabenā).

def: s.m. Deceit, trick, ruse, strategem, wile; cheat, rascality, wickedness.
more: n. m. H. pher circuit, phand snare. Ruse ; trick; wile; stratagem; cheat.

adj. & s.m. Artful, tricky, deceitful, treacherous, wicked; — an artful person, trickster, cheat, knave, &c.

more: v. n. To flutter, to wave, to twinkle, to move with convulsive motion.
v. n. To struggle, to be agitated.

def: s.f. = phaṛphaṛāhat, q.v. s.f. Flutter; struggle; disquietude, agitation.
adj. & s.m. Active, brisk, swift, quick, hasty, bustling: — a flutterer.

def: n. m. A flutterer.
n. m. Cucumis colocynthis ( indrāyan ).
n. m. The stalk of maize, etc.
s.m. A root (as ginger, &c) which separates into cloves, a race or sprig; an end or a fragment of a branch; the stalk of maize, &c.

more: n. m. The fir tree, so called from the whizzing of its branches.
s. m. The name of a tree. (Its leaves resemble those of the Cypress or of the Tamarisk, and make a whistling noise in the wind; whence the name).

def: n. m. S. स्फुर tremble. The fluttering of a bird or a flag; the tramp of a swiftly running animal. pharrāṭe bharnā , v. n. To take flight.

more: s. m. 1. A piece of bamboo. 2. A sound made by the flying of a flag or the breathing of a horse.
subs: v.n. To take flight, to fly or flee away.

more: n. m. S. परशु An axe; a hatchet.

'Tohrā phaskaute keū manā karā hau, tehūṅ phaskā le .' Mag. Wom. This woman is in her ninth month, she casts one every year.' 'Who prevents you from casting, you can cast too.'
v. n. To split, to burst, to break.

more: v. a. 1. To cause to stick (as in mud, &c.), to mire, entangle, embroil, catch, cause to be imprisoned, squash.

v.t. = phasānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to stick (as in mud, &c.); to cause to be imprisoned; to involve (one in difficulties, &c.); to entangle, ensnare, embroil, catch; to squash; — to take in, deceive, cheat

more: n. f. A handful of grain exacted for each load of grain brought to market; a similar quantity presented by school boys to their master, by women to Siva, etc.

v.n. = phaṅsnā, q.v.

v. n. 1. To be produced plentifully. 2. To become fat suddenly. 3. To be confounded with too much business. آسمان پهٿ پڙنا āsmān phaṭ paṛnā, v. n. To rain hard.

more: s. m. 1. A gate. 2. A large shutter. 3. An obstruction, impediment, a check. 4. The bar of a court of justice, where the plaintiff and defendant take their station.
subs: v.t. To put in the pound, to impound.
ex: واہگہ پر وہ دروازہ موجود ہے‘ جس کے ایک طرف کے پھاٹک بھارت میں اور دوسری طرف کے پاکستان میں کھلتے ہیں۔
vāhga par vo darvāza maujūd hai jis ke ek tarf ke phāṭak bhārat mẽ aur dūsrī tarf ke pākistān mẽ khulte ha͠i.
There is a gate at [the] Wagah [border] whose [frontier] gates on one side open into India and on the other side into Pakistan.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi پهاٿک (phaṭk /⁠phāṭak⁠/), from Sanskrit *फाट्टक्क (*phāṭṭakka), compound of *प्रहट्ट (*prahaṭṭa) + हट्ट (haṭṭa).

def: n. m. S. कपाट 1. A gate; door; entrance. 2. A pound ( mawe- shī-khāna ). 3. The bar of a court of justice, where the plaintiff and defendant take their station. 4. A bar. See پـﮩـارَ (6). phāṭak-bandī . Custody ( havālāt ); imprisonment. phāṭak-dār . A door-keeper; a pound-keeper. phāṭak meṅ dākhil karnā , v. a. To impound.
s. (dakh.) Alum ( Harris ).
s. m. (dakh.) A cracker (firework: Harris ).

ety: phaṭak˚ = Prk. फट्टक्क(इ), or फट्टक्क(इ); S. स्फट+कृ
def: v.t. To winnow; to sift, to strain; to dust, to shake or knock off any light thing which slightly adheres (as dust from a table, or crumbs from a table-cloth); — v.n. To interfere, intermeddle ( = daḵẖal denā, usually with negative adverb); to be separated; to keep or hold aloof (from), to fight shy (of); — to pay a flying visit.
more: 1. v. a. To winnow. 2. To dust, to shake or knock off any light thing which slightly adheres (as crumbs from a table-cloth or dust from a table). 3. v. n. To be separated. 4. s. m. The tape in a pellet bow which strikes the ball.

def: s.m. The tape in a pellet bow which strikes the ball.

v. a. S. स्फुट to break. 1. To sift; winnow; separate the husk from the corn. 2. To dust; to shake or knock off any light thing which slightly adheres (as crumbs from a table-cloth or dust from a table). 3. To look in; flit; stay for a short time; to pay a short visit. Sūp sī ḍāṛhī lagā-kar She/?/kh jī, Aksar auqāt ā phaṭakte haiṅ . Saudā. Just like a winnowing fan his beard so queer Oft times doth come and wag this ancient seer.
oblique plural of پھاٹَک (phāṭak)

n. f. Husk; chaff. phaṭakvā'nā , v. a. Caus. of phaṭaknā , q. v.
s.m. See s.v. phaṭ and phiṭ. s.m. Division; division of revenue, assessment among the sharers in joint tenantry; — breadth.
more: s. f. Curse, malediction, removing something to a distance.

def: s.f. Sulphate of alumina, alum.

v.t. See s.v. phaṭ and phiṭ. s.m. Division; division of revenue, assessment among the sharers in joint tenantry; — breadth.

v.n. = phaṭnā, q.v.; — and v.t. = phaṭānā, q.v.
more: v. n. To be torn, to be broken, split, rent, torn asunder, &c.

ety: phaṭ˚ = Prk. फट्ट(इ); S. स्फद्य(ते); rt. स्फट्
def: v.n. To be torn, be split, be rent, be cracked, be broken; to tear, split, crack, burst, break; to be separated, be detached; to be dispersed (as clouds); to be clarified (as milk, oil, &c., the impure part being separated from the pure); to turn, become sour (as milk); to break out (as sores); — to tremble (with fear), break into a tremor
more: v. n. To be torn, split, rent, broken, cracked, to burst, split.
subs: v.n. (intensive) To burst open; to be produced plentifully or copiously; to be bursting (as it were) with fat, &c.; to become fat suddenly; — to be overworked, to be confounded with too much business
v.n. intensive of, and = phaṭnā, q.v.

def: v. n. S. स्फुटन, Pālī. phuṭanam to burst. 1. To be torn, split, cracked, broken. Phaṭe meṅ pāoṅ, daftar meṅ nāoṅ . Prov. He makes one in a row, on the rolls he is now. (He is forsooth an officer of the court, for his name is on the rolls— as a witness ). 2. To be separated, detached; to come off. 3. To be dispersed (clouds), cleared (the sky). 4. To turn or become sour (as milk) 5. To disagree with; turn from; to be alienated. Nīke kām ko nā chaleṅ, buran ko har dam ūdh, Tulsī, aise nalan se man phāṭe jas dūdh . Dohā. For good he never stirs, for evil he's too ready all, Like milk that's turned my mind doth turn, Tulsi, from such a thrall. 6. To burst; break out (as a sore). 7. To fear; shake with fear. phaṭ paṛnā , v. n. To be produced plentifully. phaṭe meṅ pāoṅ aṛā'nā , v. n. 1. To stand in the gap or breach. 2. To burn one's finger in another's quarrel. 3. To interfere; intermeddle. phaṭā purānā , n. m. Cast-off clothes; rags. Phaṭā, purānā, ṭāṭ, kambal ke chane . Street cry. phaṭā dūdh , n. m. Spoiled milk; whey. phaṭāo , n. m. Separation. Chār pīṛhī kā phaṭāo hai .
v.t. To beat, shake, or flap (the wings, as birds about to fly); — v.n. To give forth a dull or creaking sound (as the shoe of a person walking).
more: v. a. 1. To shake or flap or clap the wings as birds just going to fly. 2. To give a sound as the shoes of a person walking.

s.m. (local) = patthar, q.v. s.m. Stone, rock; precious stone, gem; hailstone; anything hard, troublesome, trying, intolerable, &c.; — adj. Hard; hard-hearted; heavy; dull; difficult; disagreeable, intolerable, &c.

subs: s.m. One who plays at stone-throwing; one who is fond of, or is addicted to, throwiug stones; — patthar-bāzī, s.f. Stone-throwing; fondness for stone-throwing
v.n. The melting of a stony heart; a difficult task to be facilitated
(-par), Stones to fall (upon); to hail; to be crushed under a shower of stones or hailstones; to be overwhelmed with trouble, &c.; to be injured, ruined or destroyed
v.n. A stony heart to be softened or melted
s.m. A breaker of stones; — the woodpecker, Picus
(-par), To throw stones (at), to pelt with stones
patthar-tale hāth ānā or dabnā = patthar tale dāman dabnā
v.n. To be entangled in difficulties
s.m. One who is attached to the very stones of his house and who clings to it even in distress; a skinflint; a kind of serpent; a sort of fish; a sort of pot-herb
v.t. To put (a knife, &c.), to the grindstone or hone; to whet or sharpen (a knife, &c.)
(or ćhātī par patthar rakhnā), v.n. To be patient by compulsion, or from being without remedy, to suffer patiently
v.n. lit. 'To carry stones'; to labour hard
v.t. To give a rude or sharp answer; to reply rashly without understanding the meaning of one's question; to give a blunt refusal
v.t. lit. 'To break one's own head with a stone'; to teach or instruct a fool
s.m. Stony or hard heart; hard-heartedness
adj. lit. 'A line engraved on stone'; ineffaceable, ineradicable, unalterable

v.n. To become stone, to be petrified ( = pathrānā); to become hard as stone, to become rigid; to be heavy or burdensome; to be unmerciful; to receive no impression, to be impervious (to); to be fixed or immovable, to be still as a statue, be stable, stand still; to freeze, to congeal; to be stone-blind; to become useless or unserviceable.

def: s.m. = phāʼoṛā, q.v. s.m. A mattock, spade.

ety: pheṅk˚ = Prk. पेकव(इ) or पेंख(इ) = S. प्रेक्ष्य(ति), future of प्र+इष्
def: v.t. To throw, fling, cast, hurl, pitch; to dart; to let fly (a hawk, &c. at game); to set (a horse) off at full speed, to gallop; to ride full tilt (at, -par); to shed, spill, pour out; to brandish, flourish (a stick or cudgel); to throw away, squander, &c. (in these senses the more common form is pheṅk-denā)
more: v. n. 1. To throw; cast; fling; toss; hurl; shed; spill; pour out. Pāsā pheṅknā . 2. To dart; let fly; shoot; discharge. 3. To brandish; whirl; flourish. Paṭā pheṅknā . 4. To set on; urge; drive at (a hawk, etc, at game). 5. To waste; lavish; dissipate; throw away; lose. 6. To set at nought; disregard; slight; make light of. pheṅk denā , v. n. To throw away; fling away.
v. a. To throw, to fling, to dart, to let fly (a hawk, &c. at game), to set off (a horse) at full speed, to gallop.
subs: v.t. (intensive of pheṅknā), To throw away, fling away; to waste, squander, lavish; to slight, disregard, make light of; to fly (a hawk after, -par, game); to ride (a horse) full tilt (at, -par).
wety: Inherited from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *𑀨𑁂𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀇 (*phekkaï). Cognate with Gujarati ફેંકવું (pheṅkvũ), Marathi फेकणे (phekṇe).

def: s.m. Turning, twisting; turn, twist, coil, fold, bend, curvature, winding, meander, maze; crookedness, obliquity; return; circumference, round, circuitousness, distance round; equivocation, ambiguity; change; variation; difference; discrepancy, deficiency; loss, damage; alternation, revolution, vicissitude; reverse, misfortune; difficulty, perplexity, straits, dilemma
more: n. m. 1. See اُلجهاؤ (1 to 4, & 7). 2. Turning; turn; bend; winding. Rājā aur daryā kā kis ne pher pāyā? Prov. The turn of a river and the diplomacy of kings are both inscrutable. 3. Return; change; revolution; circuit; round. Āj ham ne baṛā pher khāyā . 4. Crook; crookedness; twist; obliquity; taint. Mālā pherat jug gayā, gayā na man kā pher, Kar kā mankā chhoṛ-ke, man kā mankā pher . Dohā. In telling beads a whole life spent, unchanged the crook inbred, Leave turning beads alone, turn o'er your mind instead. 5. Difficulty; perplexity; uncertainty; dilemmā. Baṛe pher meṅ paṛe hain . 6. Difference ( antar ). 7. Variation; change; discrepancy; deficiency. 8. Loss; damage; deprivation. Pher qismat kā . What next upon your fate depends. pher baṅdhnā , v. n. To occur periodically. pher badal. Vice versa; mutatis mutandis. pher boṭī , n. f. The punch and abdomen. pher paṛnā, yā khānā , v. n. …

(from پهيرنا) s. m. 1. Turning, turn, return, change, meander, maze, curvature, twisting, coil, fold, circumference. 2. Equivocation, ambiguity. 3. Difficulty, distance. 4. adv. Again, back. پهيرپڙنا pher paṛnā, To differ. پهيرپهار pher-phār, adj. Alternate. پهيرپهارکرنا pher-phār karnā, To alternate. پهيردينا pher denā, To return, to restore, refund. پهيرمين ڐالنا pher meṅ ḍālnā, To throw obstacles in the way of another. پهيرکهانا pher khānā, 1. To wind (as a river), to meander. 2. To go round about. 3. To meet with perplexities.
subs: v.n. To be round about or circuitous; — a difference or discrepancy to arise, to differ, to be discrepant or deficient
s.m. (intensive and frequentative of pher), Constant or repeated change; frequent turns or windings, &c.; alternations, vicissitudes; — adj. Alternate; — pher-phār karnā, v.n. To alternate, &c.
v.n. To wind (as a river, road, &c.), to meander; to go a roundabout way, to make a circuit; to experience a reverse of fortune, to meet with difficulties
or pher-meṅ paṛnā (-ke), To fall into the whirl or rotation (of); to fall into straits or difficulties, be involved in misfortune
v.t. To involve (one) in difficulties, place one in a dilemma or straits; to throw obstacles in the way (of another); to mislead.

ety: phera = pher, q.v.+Prk. अओ = S. कः
def: s.m. Turning; round, circuit; perambulation; walking (of the bride and bridegroom) round (the sacred fire); returning, return; alternating, alternation; — a roll; — a wooden frame with which lime, sand, &c. is measured (so called from its being passed over the lime, &c.)
more: n. m. 1. Circuit; perambulation; walk. 2. Coming; dropping in; visiting; visit. pherā phārī , n. f. See ايراپهيري (1.).
(from پهيرنا) s. m. 1. Turning, circuit, perambulation. 2. A roll. 3. A wooden frame with which lime, sand, &c. is measured.
subs: pherā-pherī, s.f. Frequent change, &c. ( = pher-phār); change, exchange, interchange; returning, alternating, alternation; walking backwards and forwards; going and coming repeatedly, visiting, visits
(-ke), To lead (a bride) round (the sacred fire), to marry, give in marriage
(-ke), To be led round (the sacred fire), to be married.

jā'o i‘t̤irāf kā aik pherā lagā ā'o.

def: v.t. = phirānā (the correct form).

adj. & s.m. Fraudulent, cratty; malicious, noxious, injurious; — a rogue, a cheat; a jackal; a rākshas, a demon.

n. m. 1. pl. of پهيرا q. v. 2. See بهانور phere ḍāl'nā , v. a. To marry; give in marriage. phere honā, yā paṛnā , v. n. To be bound in wedlock. Subh ghaṛī, subh din hue jab phere, Saiyān ne merī bāṅh gahī re . Wom. Song.

s.f. Going round; circumambulation (round an object = pradakshiṇā; parikramā); circuit, round; hawking; the rounds of a pedlar, or of a wandering mendicant; the profession of a wandering beggar, mendicancy; — slice or cut round, joint of a sugar-cane, &c.); — shift, artifice, trick, subtlety, finesse
more: n. f. 1. Circuit; round. 2. Hawking. 3. Mendicancy; begging. Jogī kī sī pherī . Prov. Like mendicants' visits. (Said to one who pays short visits and seldom). 3. Skill; mother wit; art; trick; finesse. Wom. ' Dekh rāṅḍoṅ kā chālā, sir mundā, mūṅh kālā !' Man. ' Dekh mardoṅ kī pherī, māṅ terī ki merī? ' 'See woman's fertile wit, a shaven head, face black!' 'Whose mother, yours or mine? Of man's wit there's no lack.' ☞ It is related that in an encounter of woman's wit against man's, the wife, feigning to be ill unto death, said to her husband—'Bring me your mother's ( apnī mā ke ) hair, or I will surely, die!' The husband went straightway and repeated the very words to his wife's mother. Moved by natural affection she made the cruel sacrifice demanded of her. pherī phirnā, yā karnā , v. n. To hawk; beg from door to door. pherī-wālā , n. m. A hawker; pedlar.
s. f. (dakh.) Joint, joint (of a stalk, Harris ), round or slice (of sugar cane).
subs: v.n. To go round hawking, to hawk; to go round begging
s.m. A hawker, pedlar ( = bisātī; phaṛīyā).

def: s. m. A pedler.

ety: pher˚ = Prk. फेर(इ) or फेरे(इ) = S. पर्ये(ति), rt. इ with परि
def: v.t. To turn, twist, bend; to turn round, whirl, twirl; to turn back, turn away, reject, send back; to return, give back, restore, refund; to turn one (from, or against, -kī t̤araf-se); to bring or draw one (over to one's side, -kī t̤araf); to turn over, invert, reverse, turn inside out; to alter, change; to turn aside, ward off, avert; to go over again, repeat, revise; to move (the hand) backwards and for wards (over), pass (the hand, &c. over, -par), to feel, to stroke, fondle, caress (the word hāth being expressed, e.g. us-par hāth phero); to pass (a brush, colour, &c. over, -par), to colour, whitewash, plaster (rang, &c. being expressed, e.g. us-par rang phero); to make (a horse, &c.) walk backwards and forwards; — to practise evasion, to double, shift, shuffle; to pervert, distort, or torture (another's words); to contradict (oneself)
more: v. a. 1. To turn; whirl; turn back; turn away. Chakkī pherī, chūn kī ḍherī . Wom. Prov. The flour mill turned, a heap of flour upturned. 2. To return; give back; restore. 3. To walk backwards and forwards. Ghoṛā pher lo . 4. To turn over; turn inside out; reverse. Ghoṛā kyoṅ aṛā, pān kyoṅ saṛā? Pherā * na thā . A riddle. Du-sukhnā . Why planted the horse, why rotted was the pān? 1. He was not properly trained. 2. It was not turned over. 5. To bring round or over to one's self; to win over). Apnī taraf pher līyā . 6. To reject; send; give; pay back; refund. 7. To stroke; caress; fondle. Hāth phernā . 8. To plaster; whitewash; color ( rang phernā ). 9. To go over again; revise; rehearse. 10. To shuffle; contradict oneself; twist or pervert the meaning. Zabān mat phero, zabān se beṭā beṭī parāë ho jāte haiṅ . Prov. Withdraw not from your word, your word once passed your child another's is a…

(caus. of پهرنا) v. a. 1. To turn, to turn back, to invert, to reverse, to avert, to turn away, to make to walk backwards and forwards, to bring or carry back, to shift, to repeat. 2. To plaster, to stroke. هاتهـ پهيرنا hāth phernā, To caress, to fondle. سرپرهاتهـ پهيرنا sir par hāth phernā, To deceive by coaxing.
v. n. (dakh.) To turn, turn back, return.
subs: (intens. of phernā), v.t. To return, restore, refund; to send back, &c.
v.t. To take back; to withdraw; to reclaim; to walk (a horse, &c.) backwards and forwards; to bring (one round) or over (to one's side, kī t̤araf).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Sallow, pale, wan, faded, dim, dull, faint, light (of colour); tasteless, vapid, insipid (as food without salt, &c.); weak, watery, thin, poor, flat, jejune: dull, cheerless.
more: adj. S. फल्ग sapless. 1. Tasteless insipid; vapid. * 1. Trained. 2. Turned over. 2. Pale; sallow; dim; faint; dull. Sakht yeh hāl uske hai jī kā, Muṅh to kaṛvā hai, aur badan phīkā . Jurat. 3. Cheerless; dark; gloomy; dull; cold. Raṅg basantī chūnar, sārī, yeh bhī man ko nā bhātā, Suhāg singār pīyā bin phīko, chhatyoṅ meṅ dāh lagtā . Wom. song. Nor skirt nor mantle of Spring's hue now pleasing seem, My braided hair, my jewels rare, stale, flat misseem, My husband far—my breast aflame!
adj. 1. Weak, vapid, tasteless, insipid. 2. Pale, sallow, light (in colour).

ety: phir˚ = pher˚ = Prk. फेरे(इ) or फेर(इ) = S. पर्ये(ति), rt. इ with परि
def: v.n. To turn, go round, revolve, whirl; to circulate; to turn back, to return; to walk, walk about, walk to and fro; to wander, rove, ramble, stroll; to travel; to turn over, to roll; to turn away, to turn (from, -se), to forsake (in this sense phir-jānā is the common form); to change; to turn aside, to deviate, wander; to turn, bend, become distorted or crooked, to warp; — to go forth, or to go (to the necessary), to have a stool (usually with jhāṛā or some similar word, e.g. jhāṛā phirnā)
more: v. n. S. स्फर to start. 1. To walk; march; step; pace; tread; tramp; walk about; go round; circulate; whirl; wheel; wander; ramble; stroll; saunter; travel. Phr. To take a walk; take a turn; take a stroll; bend one's steps or course; take the air. Khojī pāṅjī ke chaurā le aulauṅ, dalyā lihalauṅ udhār, Rasoīyā kar-ke pāras lavlauṅ, piyavā phirat bajār . Bhoj. Wom. Song. Some rice I sought and found, some dāl one lent to me, I cooked the mess—my husband's out soiled doves to see! 2. To turn; return; come back. 3. To deviate; diverge; turn or draw aside. Phirūṅ jo tujh se to mujh se merā Khudā phir jāë! Wom. If I turn from you may my God turn from me! 4. To turn from; rise against; revolt; rebel; mutiny ( bāqī honā ). 1. Sārī fauj bādshāh se phir gaī . 2. Ek phirne se tere, sārā zāmānā phir gāyā . 4. To turn aside from one's intention; evade; shuffle; prevaricate. Phir be ghoṛe yahīṅ se ! P…
(perhaps, from Sans. परिक्रम Going round) v. n. To turn, return, walk about, go round, circulate, roll, whirl, to wheel, wander, travel, ramble, change, revolt.
subs: (-se), To turn away from (in dissatisfaction or displeasure), to be dissatisfied, be displeased

def: s.m. A flower, a blossom; — a boss, knob, stud, bunch of ribands, rosette, cockade; — the menses (of which children are the blossoms)
more: n. m. S. फुल्ल a full-blown flower. 1. A flower; a blossom. Kabhī phūl kī chhaṛī bhī nahīṅ mārī . 2. A boss; knob, stud; rosette. 3. A spark. Chirāg meṅ se phūl jhaṛte haiṅ . 4. The menses. Har mahīne meṅ kuṛhāte the mujhe phūl ke din, Bāre ab-ke to mereṭal gaë māmūl ke din . Wom. Raṅgīṅ. Each month the monthly flow to me brought grief, At last this time its stoppage brings relief. 5. The bones of a corpse after partial cremation. 6. A white metal; purified bell-metal. 7. Any light thing; a small quantity. Bhaṅg ke do phūl denā . 8. The white marks of leprosy. 9. (P1.) A Mah. ceremony performed on the third day after death. 10. The best kind of native wine. phūl, phūl sā, halkā phūl , adj. Light as a flower; very light. phūl ānā , v. n. To have the menses ( kapṛoṅ se honā ). phūl jhaṛ'nā , v. n. 1. To drop flowers of rhetoric. Bāt kyā karte haiṅ, muṅh se phūl jhaṛte haiṅ . 2. To fall fro…

s. m. 1. A flower, a blossom; boss, stud, bunch of ribands, &c. a cockade. 2. The menses, of which children are the fruit. 3. A swelling. 4. Bones of a dead person after the fleshy parts are burned. 5. Lights or fire (seen at night). 6. (in the plur.) A ceremony performed in honour of a deceased person on the third day after his death. پهول اٿهنا phūl uṭhnā, v. n. To be performed (a ceremony in honour of the dead), to complete the forty days of mourning. پهول پڙنا phūl paṛnā, v. n. To break out (a fire). پهول بيٿهنا phūl baiṭhnā, v. n. To be glad. پهول جانا phūl jānā, v. n. 1. To swell. 2. To be delighted, to be pleased. 3. To become fat. پهول جهڙ نا phūl jhaṛnā, v. n. 1. To speak eloquently. 2. To fall from a lamp (drops of burning oil).
subs: a swelling; — a spark of fire; lights or fire

(seen at night); the bones of a corpse after partial cremation; — a ceremony performed (by Musalmāns) in honour of a deceased person on the third day after his death, when a portion of the Qorʼan is recited, and flowers are placed on trays in the midst of the assembly); — a white metal, bell-metal, electrum; — the white marks of leprosy; — a pearl or white opaque spot in the corner of the eye ( = phullī); — a maggot that is hatched in meat or in neglected sores; — a head, stick, small quantity (as we say a head of celery, — e.g. bhang-ke do phūl denā); — the best kind of native wine
v.n. The menses to come on, to have the menses
v.n. The ceremony of phūl (q.v.) to be completed; to complete the forty days of mourning
s.f. A knot of ribands, rosette, cockade
v.n. Sparks to fall (from a burning building, &c.); fire to break out
v.n. To drop flowers (of rhetoric), to speak eloquently; — drops of burning oil to fall (from a lamp); sparks of fire to fall copiously (from a burning mass)
(par), To offer flowers (at a shrine, &c.), to strew flowers (on a tomb)
s.m. A kind of Hindū sport or show
adj. (f. -ī) Like a flower; light as a flower; delicate or frail as a flower
v.n. To live on the fragrance of flowers (said of a woman who eats little)
adj. & s.m. Flowering, in flower; — flower-man
s.m. Wreath or garland of flowers; a delicate, fragile, or frail person or thing
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀨𑀼𑀮𑁆𑀮 (phulla), from Sanskrit फुल्ल (phulla). Cognates include Assamese ফুল (phul), Bengali ফুল (phul), Bhojpuri फूल (phūl), Gujarati ફૂલ (phūl), Maithili फूल (phūl), Marathi फूल (phūl), Nepali फूल (phūl), Odia ଫୁଲ (phula), Punjabi پھل / ਫੁੱਲ (phulla), and Sindhi ڦل (phulu).

def: (dakh.) Adorning with flowers.

ety: perf. part. of phūlnā; and = Prk. फुल्लिओ; S. स्फुटित+कः, rt. स्फुट्; and फुल्ल+कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Swollen, swelled, puffed up or out; — blossomed, flowered, having flowers
more: adj. 1. Swelled. 2. Blossomed. پهولانسمانا phūlā na-samānā, Not to be able to contain oneʼs self from delight, to be overjoyed, to exult.
subs: v.n. To be unable to contain oneself (from delight), to be overjoyed, to exult.

ety: rt. of phulānā, q.v.+ Prk. एअव्वअ+ट्ट = S. (इ)तव्य+क+ त्वं
def: s.f. Swelling, inflation, distension, puffiness; protuberance.

ety: caus. of phūlnā; — phulā˚ = Prk. फुल्लावे(इ) or फुल्लाव(इ) = S. स्फुट+आपि
def: v.t. To cause to swell; to swell out, puff out; to inflate, distend, expand; to fatten; (met.) to puff (with flattery), to make proud; &c. (see phūlnā).
more: v. a. See پهولنا v. n.
(trans. of پهولنا) v. a. 1. To cause to swell, to inflate, distend. 2. To fatten. 3. ( met. ) To make proud or to puff up with flattery.

def: s.f. = phunaṅg, q.v. s.f. Top, summit; peak, point, end, extremity, tip (of a bough, or of the nose, &c.); projection, anything that projects or stands prominently out.
n. f. The top; head; summit; the topmost branch.
s.m. = phulāʼo; phulāwaṭ; qq.v.
n. m. phulā'vaṭ , n. f. Swelling; protuberance.
s. A swelling.
s.m. = next, q.v.
s.m. Coaxing, wheedling; flattery, &c. ( = phuslāwā).
more: Mah. Wom. n. m. Coaxing ( phuslāvā ). Mahīne bhar se phulāsrā de rakkhā hai, jūtī nahīṅ lā dete . Wom.

def: s.f. A small swollen or puffed cake of fruit and pulse (or of pulse alone) fried in ghī or oil; fritters (syn. pakauṛī; phulkī).
more: s. f. Bread or cake made of fruit and pulse, and fried in ghī or oil.

ety: rt. of phulānā, q.v.+ Prk. एअव्वअ+ट्ट = S. (इ)तव्य+क+ त्वं
def: s.f. Swelling, inflation, distension, puffiness; protuberance.
s. (dakh.) A flowering thicket, a bed or garden of flowers.
s.m. = H پهلباڙي फुलबाड़ी phul-bāṛī, s.f. = phul-wāṛī, q.v.
s.f. = H پهلباڙ फुलबाड़ phul-bāṛ, s.m. = phul-wāṛī, q.v.
s. f. See پهلجهڙي phuljhaṛī.
